questions.text: In which state was Santosh Yadav born?
questions.explanation: Santosh Yadav was born in the small village of Joniyawas of Rewari District in Haryana.

questions.text: What does the name 'Santosh' mean?
questions.explanation: The name Santosh means contentment, though she was not always content with her place in a traditional society.

questions.text: Why was Santosh sent to the local village school?
questions.explanation: In line with the prevailing custom in the family, she was sent to the local village school.

questions.text: At what age was Santosh under pressure to get married?
questions.explanation: In her village, girls were usually married at sixteen, and she was pressured to do the same.

questions.text: Where did Santosh go for her higher studies after finishing school in Delhi?
questions.explanation: She went to Jaipur to join Maharani College.

questions.text: Which hills could Santosh see from her room in Kasturba Hostel?
questions.explanation: From her room, she used to watch villagers going up the Aravalli Hills and vanishing.

questions.text: Which institute did Santosh join for mountaineering training?
questions.explanation: She enrolled at Uttarkashi’s Nehru Institute of Mountaineering.

questions.text: How many times did Santosh Yadav scale Mt. Everest?
questions.explanation: She is the only woman in the world who has scaled Mt Everest twice.

questions.text: What honor was conferred upon Santosh by the Indian Government?
questions.explanation: The government bestowed upon her one of the nation’s top honours, the Padma Shri.

questions.text: How did Santosh feel when she was literally on top of the world?
options: It took time for the enormity to sink in
questions.explanation: She described it as an indescribable moment that took time to process.

questions.text: Santosh brought down ______ kg of garbage from the Himalayas.
questions.explanation: Being a fervent environmentalist, she collected and brought down 500 kg of garbage.

questions.text: Maria Sharapova reached the number one position in world women's tennis on:
questions.explanation: She achieved the world number one ranking on Monday, 22 August 2005.

questions.text: Maria Sharapova was born in:
questions.explanation: Maria Sharapova is a Russian girl from Siberia.

questions.text: At what age did Maria leave for the United States?
questions.explanation: She was not yet ten (9 years old) when she was packed off to train in the US.

questions.text: Who accompanied Maria to the United States?
questions.explanation: The trip to Florida with her father Yuri launched her on the path to success.

questions.text: Why couldn't Maria's mother accompany her to the US?
questions.explanation: Her mother Yelena had to stay back in Siberia due to visa restrictions.

questions.text: What did the senior tennis pupils make Maria do at night?
options: Tidy up the room and clean it
questions.explanation: The seniors would wake her up at 11 p.m. and order her to clean the room.

questions.text: Instead of letting bullying depress her, Maria became more:
questions.explanation: She learned how to take care of herself and never thought of quitting.

questions.text: Maria Sharapova won the Women's Singles at Wimbledon in:
questions.explanation: She bagged the women’s singles crown at Wimbledon in 2004.

questions.text: What is Maria's mantra for success?
questions.explanation: She works hard and is highly competitive, viewing it as her job.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a hobby of Maria Sharapova?
questions.explanation: Her hobbies are fashion, singing, and dancing, but singing is mentioned alongside dancing as 'hobbies'. Pancake cooking is also a favorite.

questions.text: What is Maria Sharapova's favorite drink?
questions.explanation: She has a fondness for sophisticated evening gowns and sparkling orange drinks.

questions.text: Maria Sharapova considers tennis as:
questions.explanation: She says, 'Tennis is a business and a sport, but the most important thing is becoming number one.'

questions.text: Despite living in the US, Maria is proud of her ______ nationality.
questions.explanation: She says her blood is totally Russian and she would play the Olympics for Russia.

questions.text: Santosh was a 'rebel' because she:
options: Wore shorts instead of traditional dresses
questions.explanation: She preferred shorts while other girls wore traditional Indian dresses.

questions.text: Santosh threatened her parents that she would never marry if she did not:
questions.explanation: She left home and got herself enrolled in a school in Delhi to ensure she got an education.

questions.text: How did Santosh's parents react when she said she'd work part-time to pay her fees?
options: They agreed to pay her fees
questions.explanation: Her parents then agreed to pay for her education in Delhi.

questions.text: The iron will and mental toughness of Santosh was evident during her:
questions.explanation: Her physical endurance and mental toughness were repeatedly proven during her climbs.

questions.text: During the 1992 Everest mission, Santosh saved the life of which climber?
questions.explanation: She shared her oxygen with Mohan Singh, saving his life.

questions.text: What was the result of Santosh's hard work during the Aravalli Hills incident?
options: She was invited by climbers to join them
questions.explanation: The climbers she met encouraged her to take to climbing.

questions.text: Maria's father worked hard in the US to:
questions.explanation: He worked as much as he could to pay for her training.

questions.text: What did Maria sacrifice to reach the top?
options: Her family life and mother's company
questions.explanation: The two-year separation from her mother was a heart-wrenching sacrifice.

questions.text: Maria believes that 'nothing comes ______'.
questions.explanation: She emphasizes that one has to pay the price for excellence.

questions.text: What does Maria like to read?
questions.explanation: She has a fondness for the novels of Arthur Conan Doyle.

questions.text: What common trait do Santosh and Maria share?
options: They are from the same country
questions.explanation: Both women showed exceptional mental strength to reach the top of their fields.

questions.text: Santosh climbed Everest for the second time within ______ months of her first climb.
questions.explanation: She was invited by an Indo-Nepalese Women’s Expedition within twelve months.

questions.text: Santosh's concern for the environment is shown by her bringing back:
questions.explanation: She brought back 500 kg of waste to help clean the mountains.

questions.text: Maria Sharapova's ascent to the top took only ______ years of professional tennis.
questions.explanation: She reached the number one spot just four years after starting her journey in professional tennis.

questions.text: Santosh was born in a family of how many brothers?
questions.explanation: She was the sixth child in a family with five sons.

questions.text: Why did Maria call herself 'a Russian'?
options: Because of her deep roots and heritage
questions.explanation: She maintains her Russian citizenship and pride despite living in the US.
